## Should fix (Warning)

- [ ] `post-compact-context.md` — 151 lines (26% over 120-line ceiling); costs tokens on every
  compaction. Five concrete compression targets with rewrites:
  (1) TDD rule (lines 47–50): 4→2 lines — "Red-Green-Refactor. Assert specific values
  (not just non-null), read back state (DB/KV) to verify side effects."
  (2) Memory Recall (lines 86–89): reformat prose → 5 bullets for post-compaction scannability.
  (3) Error Handling (lines 59–64): split into two 3-line rules (Throw vs Return + Error Messages).
  (4) Naming Conventions (lines 107–111): tighten to 2 lines: "Folders: `src/api/`,
  `services/`, `repository/`, `models/`, `lib/`. Files: PascalCase (classes), camelCase
  (modules). DB: snake_case plural tables + columns."
  (5) Prompting Quality (lines 134–138): split into two 2-line rules (Constraint Keywords,
  Constraint Budget). Target: ~120 lines total.

- [ ] `agents/01-core-development/backend-developer.md:53`,
  `agents/01-core-development/api-designer.md`,
  `agents/04-quality-security/architect-reviewer.md`,
  `agents/04-quality-security/code-reviewer.md` — Serena MCP navigation instruction
  duplicated verbatim in all four (~800 tokens/session). Fix: remove the duplicate
  paragraph from all four agents; add a single canonical entry to `rules/lsp.md` under a
  new "## Subagent note" heading; add `rules/lsp.md` to each agent's Required Rules list.

- [ ] `.mcp.json` — mem0 uses deprecated SSE transport (`"type": "sse"`). Fix: migrate to
  HTTP transport — set `"type": "http"` and update the URL to the mem0 HTTP endpoint.
  Verify the mem0 server supports HTTP before applying.

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md` — no tool-count guidance; `backend-developer.md:4` and
  `microservices-architect.md:4` each list 19 tools (>8 causes decision paralysis per
  practitioner consensus across 3 sources, 2026). Fix: add an anti-pattern row to the
  Anti-patterns table: `| Tool list >8 per agent | Decision paralysis; wasted token
  selection | Scope to 3–5 tools per agent; delegate to narrower specialists |`

- [ ] `hooks/session-start.sh:24–33` — auto-installs ast-grep via brew/apt on every
  SessionStart without user opt-in; violates idempotency. Fix: wrap the install block in
  an opt-in guard: `if [[ "${CLAUDE_AUTO_INSTALL_TOOLS:-false}" == "true" ]]; then ... fi`.
  Add a note to README about setting the flag.

- [ ] `skills/i18n-setup/scripts/translate.ts:138` — hardcodes `model: 'claude-opus-4-6'`
  (stale model). Fix: `model: process.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL ?? 'claude-opus-4-8'`. Also
  update the comment at line 4.

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md` — no cost-justification preamble for multi-agent dispatch.
  Fix: add ≤4 lines before the "plan before executing" block:
  "Multi-agent orchestration costs ~15× more tokens than single-agent dispatch.
  Justify multi-agent when: (1) parallel bottleneck demonstrated, (2) domain/compliance
  isolation required, (3) cognitive boundary needed. Default to single-agent; split only
  when a specific bottleneck is demonstrated."

## Consider improving (Suggestion)

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:27–58` — subagent prompt structure (8 sections) missing an
  explicit Boundaries tier. Fix: add a 9th section after "Quality bar":
  "**Boundaries** — three tiers: *Always do* (non-negotiables), *Ask first* (actions
  requiring approval), *Never do* (hard stops). Omit if all three tiers are empty."
  (Practitioner consensus, 3 sources, 2026; High applicability to Claude subagents.)

- [ ] `rules/prompting-quality.md:3–14` — verbose preamble, 75→40 words (47% compression).
  Replace lines 3–14 with:
  "Use at least one constraint keyword per non-trivial request:
  - Scope: "only", "limit to", "restrict to", "just"
  - Prohibitions: "do not", "avoid", "never", "without"
  - Requirements: "must", "always", "ensure", "require"
  - Format: "in under N lines", "as a table", "no prose""
  Delete the trailing 3-sentence paragraph (duplicates the list).

- [ ] `rules/observability.md:3–14` — verbose preamble, 80→38 words (53% compression).
  Replace lines 3–14 with:
  "Before implementation: define 'working' as measurable SLI + SLO for each operation.
  - SLI: metric (error rate, latency p95, queue depth)
  - SLO: threshold triggering page (e.g., error rate < 0.1%, p95 < 200ms)
  Cannot define SLI? Feature not ready to build — stop."

- [ ] `rules/code-principles.md:9–11` — YAGNI opening paragraph restates its own bullets.
  Replace lines 9–11 with: "YAGNI governs design decisions, not handed requirements
  (spec fidelity, ports, translations)."

- [ ] `rules/architecture.md:51–67` — fitness functions section, 110→45 words (59%
  compression). Replace lines 51–67 with:
  "Fitness functions: automated tests verifying architectural invariants in CI.
  Examples: 'no cross-handler imports', 'auth middleware on all handlers', 'no direct
  DB access outside repository/'.
  Express every architectural constraint as a lint/import check/test — not code review."

- [ ] `rules/extended-thinking.md:1–6` — blockquote deprecation notice duplicates the
  directive at lines 35–40. Fix: delete lines 1–6; the `Do NOT use budget_tokens`
  directive at line 40 is the authoritative form.

- [ ] `agents/01-core-development/api-designer.md` — dead content: line 183 is a canned
  placeholder delivery statement; lines 69–78 are generic performance topic labels with
  no actionable content. Fix: delete both blocks.

- [ ] Cross-skill model routing: 16+ skills lack explicit model routing tables (analytics-
  setup, auth-setup, doc-pdf, doc-xlsx, explore, file-organizer, internal-comms,
  powerpoint-addin-setup, project-bootstrap, review-pr, sandbox, video-downloader and
  others). Fix: add a one-line routing note to each skill's preamble: "Model routing:

- [ ] 8 skills write TypeScript but don't run `npx tsc --noEmit` in verification:
  analytics-setup, auth-setup, compliance-setup, i18n-setup, payments-setup,
  powerpoint-addin-setup, testing-setup, upgrade-deps. Fix: add `npx tsc --noEmit` to
  each skill's verify/quality-gate step. (Carried from 2026-05-29 — still unaddressed.)

- [ ] `rules/parallelism.md:49` — Interface contracts section specifies what data is
  exchanged but not format. Fix: add one sentence: "If subagent output is consumed by a
  downstream agent, specify a JSON schema. If output is human-facing, prose is appropriate."

- [ ] No `effort:` frontmatter in any agent definition. Fix: add `effort: high` to
  `agents/04-quality-security/security-auditor.md`,
  `agents/04-quality-security/compliance-auditor.md`,

## Inline comments to add (Notes)

- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` — move the "Agent Memory" section (lines ~80–82) to immediately after
  the "Verification" section; memory search is a precondition for every task and should
  appear early.

- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` — add under the "Agents" section: "Use Workflow (via `Workflow` tool) for
  multi-step parallel orchestration with deterministic control flow. Prefer Agent tool for
  individual specialist delegation. Workflow is user-opt-in only — never invoke unless
  the user explicitly requests it or a skill instructs it."

- [ ] `testing.md` — add a Coverage Exclusions note after the 90/90/90 rule: "Exclude:
  generated code, schema migrations, one-off scripts, pure config files, and test helper
  files in test/helpers/."

- [ ] `rules/error-handling.md` — "timeout configured" lacks default guidance. Add:
  "Reasonable defaults: 5s for synchronous API calls, 30s for batch/long-running
  operations, 1s for cache reads. Make the timeout configurable via env var or options."
